summary: Excellent soundboard capture of a mellow June ’76 comeback show that shines on Crazy Fingers, Help>Slip, Scarlet Begonias and a gritty Dancin’ performance.
review: The recording is a clean, studio‑like soundboard tape with no reported flaws. Musically the band delivers a relaxed yet exploratory set – Jerry’s inspired playing, Keith’s genius keyboards, and standout jams on Crazy Fingers, Help>Slip, Scarlet Begonias, Let It Grow and an especially gritty Dancin’. Reviewers uniformly praise the performance as one of the band’s finest early‑summer ’76 shows.

ai_rating.rationale: All four reviews are 4–5 stars, highlighting exceptional playing and a flawless recording; the weighted average yields a rating in the 4.5‑4.7 range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No specific technical complaints; reviewers describe the tape as studio‑like and crystal clear.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: A mellow, exploratory set that flows smoothly with extended jams (Scarlet, Help>Slip) and finishes on a gritty, funky Dancin' that many reviewers call a highlight.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Described as inspired; his tone on Dancin' is gritty and dirty, sounding amazing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Called "just right" in the overall mix of the band.
band_member_comments.Bob: Also noted as "just right" alongside Phil.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith is praised for being in full genius mode; Donna’s vocals are said to be on point.
